title: Custard creams
subtitle: Custard powder biscuits with vanilla custard buttercream filling
excerpt: Custard creams use custard powder in both the biscuit and the filling. The biscuits are rolled thin, cut, and baked pale — they should barely colour. The custard buttercream goes in while the biscuits are completely cold to stop it melting.
sourceNotes: Custard creams became one of the most popular British biscuits from the early twentieth century. The biscuit and filling both using custard powder (a product introduced by Alfred Bird in 1837) is the characteristic British form, widely documented in domestic cookbooks from the 1900s onward.

recipe.batchNotes: Unfilled biscuits keep for 2 weeks in a tin. Fill on the day of serving for the crispest texture.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Store unfilled biscuits in an airtight tin for up to 2 weeks. Filled biscuits are best on the day.

glossaryTerms.definition: A cornflour-based powder flavoured with vanilla, used to make instant custard sauce when mixed with milk. In biscuit baking, it replaces some of the flour to give a finer crumb and a mild custard flavour. It also contributes to the distinctive pale yellow colour of custard cream biscuits.

content.text: The pale yellow colour of custard creams comes from
in the dough. Roll the biscuits to 3 mm and bake just until set, not browned. The genuine custard cream has a crisp, dry bite; colour in the oven kills that.
Make dough, chill, roll, and bake

Beat {{unsalted-butter}} and {{icing-sugar}} together until light. Add {{vanilla-extract}} and {{whole-milk}}. Stir in {{plain-flour}} and {{custard-powder}} until a dough forms. Wrap and chill for 30 minutes. Heat the oven to 180°C (160°C fan). Roll on a lightly floured surface to 3 mm thick. Cut into rectangles (about 4 × 6 cm) or use any cutter. Place on lined trays and bake for 12–14 minutes until just set and barely coloured at the edges. Cool completely on a wire rack before filling.

Beat the filling {{unsalted-butter}}, {{icing-sugar}}, {{custard-powder}}, and {{vanilla-extract}} until smooth and pale. Spread a small amount on the flat side of half the biscuits. Press a second biscuit on top, flat-side down, with just enough pressure to push the filling to the edges. Leave for 30 minutes for the filling to firm.
